content.title: Who Owns a Child's Eid Salami Money?
content.subtitle: Can parents or guardians spend this money on household expenses?
content.body:
🎁 Who Owns a Child’s Eid Salami Money?

In Bangladesh, it is common for children to receive Eid Salami (gift money) during Eid. They may also receive money as gifts from relatives and well-wishers on various occasions. In most cases, parents take responsibility for managing these funds. But what does Islamic law say about the ownership and use of this money? Let’s find out.

⚜️ Who is the Owner of the Salami Money?

The money a child receives as Eid Salami or as a gift on any other occasion belongs to that child. From an Islamic perspective, even a minor child can own wealth and property. Therefore, parents or guardians do not become the owners of the money simply because they are responsible for the child.

💰 Can Parents Spend This Money?

Parents are not allowed to spend their child’s Salami or gift money for their own personal needs or household expenses. This is because the rightful owner of the money is the child.

✅ When Can This Money Be Used?

The money may be spent for the child's clear benefit, such as education, medical treatment, or other necessary expenses. However, proper records should be maintained so that it is clear how much money belonged to each child and how much was spent on their behalf.

Parents may also choose to save and preserve the money separately, so that it can be handed over to the child when they become mature enough to understand and manage it responsibly.

Let us treat our children's Eid Salami and gifted money as a trust (amanah) and fulfill its rights in accordance with Islamic guidance.

May Allah grant us the ability to understand and practice these rulings correctly. Ameen.

Relevant References:

  • Fatawa al-Shami 5/689

  • Tabyin al-Haqa'iq 5/96

  • Durar al-Hukkam 1258

  • Fatawa al-Hindiyyah 4/383, 2088

  • Fatawa Mazahir al-Ulum 1/319

  • Jamia Uloom-ul-Islamia Binori Town — Fatwa No. 1437112000013
